components.name: Angular SPA Frontend
components.description: Angular-based single-page application served from frontend/dist/frontend/. Handles all user-facing UI, stores JWT tokens in localStorage, and communicates with the Express backend via REST and Socket.IO. Contains intentional client-side vulnerabilities including DOM XSS, insecure localStorage token storage, and prototype pollution.
components.technology: Angular 19+, TypeScript, Socket.IO client, localStorage JWT

components.name: Express.js REST API Backend
components.description: Node.js 24 / Express 4.22.1 monolith serving REST APIs, static files, and challenge logic. Contains intentional vulnerabilities including SQL injection (login, search), JWT signing with hardcoded RSA private key (lib/insecurity.ts), eval() SSTI (userProfile.ts), SSRF (profileImageUrlUpload.ts), and 21 endpoints missing authentication middleware.
components.technology: Node.js 24, Express 4.22.1, express-jwt 0.1.3, jsonwebtoken 0.4.0, helmet 4.6.0, morgan

components.name: File Upload & Processing Service
components.description: Express route handlers responsible for file ingestion and processing: multer-based multipart upload (fileUpload.ts), profile image upload from URL (profileImageUrlUpload.ts), profile image from file (profileImageFileUpload.ts). Contains XXE via libxmljs2 with noent:true, SSRF via unchecked URL fetch, and path traversal via logfileServer/keyServer/quarantineServer.

components.name: B2B Order Processing API
components.description: Machine-to-machine API endpoint at POST /b2b/v2/orders protected by JWT bearer auth. Processes JSON order payloads via notevil sandbox (routes/b2bOrder.ts:23) with vm.runInContext — susceptible to sandbox escape RCE. Accepts arbitrary JavaScript expressions as orderLinesData.

components.name: Data Layer (SQLite + MarsDB)
components.description: Persistence layer composed of SQLite 3 via Sequelize 6.37.3 ORM for relational user/product/order data, MarsDB 0.6.11 for in-memory NoSQL storage (chatbot/memories), and local filesystem for uploaded files, encryption keys, and FTP downloads. SQL injection paths exist at the Sequelize raw-query level (login, search). The encryption keys directory (encryptionkeys/) is served publicly.
components.technology: SQLite 3, Sequelize 6.37.3, MarsDB 0.6.11, sqlite3 5.1.7, local filesystem

components.name: Authentication & Session Surface
components.description: Login, token/session issuance, password-reset and MFA handlers (lib/insecurity.ts, lib/startup/registerWebsocketEvents.ts, routes/2fa.ts, routes/authenticatedUsers.ts, …). The credential-bearing entry surface — the highest-value authentication boundary in the system.

components.name: CI/CD Pipeline
components.description: Continuous-integration / deployment workflows (.github/workflows/ci.yml, .github/workflows/codeql-analysis.yml, .github/workflows/frontend-bundle-analysis.yml, .github/workflows/lint-fixer.yml, …). Build and release automation holding repository, registry and deploy credentials, with supply-chain reach into the produced artifact.

components.name: Real-time WebSocket Channel
components.description: Server-side socket.io real-time channel pushing live events to connected browsers. Internet-facing WebSocket surface with its own connection auth/origin checks and message-handler trust boundary.
